summary: Automatically synthesises a code execution harness from task specifications using iterative code refinement; smaller model with synthesised harness outperforms larger model without one on 145 TextArena games.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly maps to adding a HarnessGeneration stage that synthesises failing Vitest tests from plan acceptance criteria before the Implementing stage runs.

summary: Multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code CLI that decomposes tasks into parallel sub-tasks across independent file clusters running in separate git worktrees, achieving 2-3x throughput on complex tasks.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly addresses limitation #2 (no parallel agent execution); model for parallel sub-agent implementation within a single issue's Implementing stage.

source: LAMaS: Learning Latency-Aware Orchestration for Parallel Multi-Agent Systems
summary: Framework enabling parallel multi-agent execution with explicit latency supervision that reduces critical path length by 38-46% compared to sequential baselines while maintaining or improving task performance.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — formalises the critical-path reduction achievable by HID-136's parallel worktree approach; informed the technical justification for the issue.

summary: Coding agent framework that edits its own prompts and tools based on execution feedback, achieving self-improvement without human intervention through iterative benchmark evaluation.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — maps to evolving static stage prompts in promptRenderer.ts based on accumulated StageOutcome data; directly addresses the lack of any feedback loop on prompt quality.

source: MemRL: Self-Evolving Agents via Runtime Reinforcement Learning on Episodic Memory
summary: Non-parametric framework that enables agents to self-evolve via runtime RL on episodic memory using a two-phase retrieval mechanism (semantic relevance + Q-value utility); no model weight changes required.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — addresses limitation #8 (no agent memory across runs), but this angle is already covered by HID-134 (cross-run execution-trace memory via RL-scored retrieval) created in a prior run.

source: PASTE: Pattern-Aware Speculative Tool Execution (Microsoft Research)
summary: Hides tool latency by predicting and pre-executing likely tool-call sequences during LLM thinking time; reduces average task completion time 48.5% and improves tool throughput 1.8x.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — conceptually similar to HID-101 (speculative implementation skeleton during plan-review idle window) already in the backlog; direct PASTE adaptation is hard for CLI subprocess model where prompt depends on previous stage output.

summary: Open-ended self-improving system that iteratively modifies its own code using an evolutionary archive of agents; improves SWE-bench from 20% to 50% through autonomous self-modification.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — too architecturally radical for a 1-2 sprint issue; requires full agent meta-programming infrastructure. Interesting for long-term roadmap.

source: BAGEN: Are LLM Agents Budget-Aware? / BATS Budget-Aware Test-time Scaling
summary: Framework introducing explicit resource limits into agentic systems (token budgets, tool-use limits, monetary cost caps) with budget-aware planning and adaptive stopping rules.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — addresses limitation #6, but already covered by HID-79, HID-100, HID-121, HID-124, and HID-129 in the backlog.

summary: AI agent that runs ML experiments in a loop, keeping only changes that beat current best result; the Karpathy Loop: agent modifies code, trains for 5 minutes, checks result, keeps or discards, repeats.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — a fascinating autonomous research loop but targets ML experiment optimisation not software engineering task orchestration; AgentForge is already the equivalent loop for code tasks.

source: Graceful Degradation Patterns for AI Agent Systems (Zylos Research)
summary: Layered resilience architecture (circuit breakers, fallback chains, bulkheads, self-healing state machines) for production AI agent systems; multi-agent systems fail at 41-86.7% without deliberate fault tolerance.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — directly addresses limitation #5, but already covered by HID-132, HID-126, HID-120, HID-117, HID-115, HID-111, HID-108, HID-105, and HID-90 in the backlog.

source: NVIDIA ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training
summary: Decoupled rollout orchestration service for RL training of multi-turn LLM agents; async three-stage assembly line (INIT/RUN/EVAL) as standalone HTTP service separate from the training loop.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — interesting for future RL-based orchestration policy optimisation, but requires RL training infrastructure not present in AgentForge; too early given current architecture.

summary: 2026 industry-standard approaches for per-stage cost attribution, token tracking per span, and LLM observability platforms (Langfuse, Langwatch, Helicone) for production agent systems.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — addresses limitation #4, but already covered by HID-133, HID-118, HID-83, and HID-52 in the backlog.

summary: Standardised HTTP protocol for direct agent-to-agent communication enabling heterogeneous agents to discover, negotiate, and execute collaborative workflows; becoming de-facto standard with 1M+ registered agents projected by end 2026.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — could enable structured typed task cards for Claude→Codex→Claude stage handoffs and future agent swappability, but HTTP-based A2A doesn't map cleanly to stdin-based CLI subprocess model; too early.

source: DARWIN: Dynamic Agentically Rewriting Self-Improving Network
summary: Network that dynamically rewrites its own orchestration configuration based on performance feedback; modifies routing and orchestration policies rather than agent code itself.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — auto-updating policyEngine.ts rules based on observed outcomes is compelling but architecturally risky; goes against explicit determinism principle. Rejected in favour of safer SICA-style prompt evolution.

source: Nexus: Execution-Grounded Multi-Agent Test Oracle Synthesis
summary: Multi-agent system that synthesises test oracles grounded in actual execution traces, making tests more reliable than specification-only approaches.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — complementary to AutoHarness; grounds harness tests in execution traces rather than static spec. Selected AutoHarness (HID-135) instead as the primary test synthesis issue; Nexus approach could extend HID-135 in a follow-on.

source: OpenAI Harness Engineering Blog Post (Ryan Lopopolo)
summary: Defines harness engineering as the practice of fixing the agent environment — context before planning, constraints during execution, verification feedback loops. Shipped production app with zero manually written code lines using constraint-first approach.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — informed the framing of HID-135; constraint-first principle (synthesise constraints before implementation) is exactly what HarnessGeneration implements.
